page: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 1 physical AMD EPYC core with hyper-threading. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized performance. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top tier, whereas multi-core performance falls into the average tier. Memory bandwidth is mixed, with small-block read-only operations performing weakly, while large-block read and write operations maintain average throughput. Passmark metrics show weak performance in CPU physics, encryption, and extended instructions. Database operations and static web serving throughput are poor, placing in the bottom 10% of tested instances.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. However, the shared CPU allocation and poor database and web serving benchmarks present clear tradeoffs for high-concurrency production environments. It is best suited for memory-bound tasks with moderate compute requirements, such as caching layers, lightweight data processing, or small-scale memory-optimized applications.
description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 2 shared vCPUs on a single physical AMD EPYC core, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core CPU capability that ranks in the top tier, contrasted by average multi-core performance and weak memory bandwidth for small blocks. Database operations and static web serving throughput are poor, reflecting the limitations of its shared CPU allocation. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-concurrency workloads, making it suitable for caching, lightweight data processing, and memory-optimized development environments.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-200s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 2 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core speed but limited database and web serving throughput.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-200s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-heavy, low-concurrency tasks.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Poor database operations and static web serving throughput
Best suited for memory-bound tasks, caching, and lightweight data processing
